title: Wrong result for dot matching newline with REGEXP
description:
https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/5.7/en/regexp.html
https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/8.0/en/regexp.html
The following example returns 1, not 0:

SELECT 'new*\n*line' REGEXP 'new\\*.\\*line';
How to repeat:
See description
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, text_column VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, num_column INTEGER DEFAULT 0);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, text_column, num_column) VALUES (1, 'new*\n*line', 10);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, text_column, num_column) VALUES (2, 'another*\n*example', NULL);
SELECT id, REGEXP_CONTAINS(text_column, 'new\*.*line') AS match_result FROM test_table WHERE num_column IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE REGEXP_CONTAINS(text_column, '.*') AND num_column < 20;
key_bug_features: Dot matching newline in REGEXP fails
